findings.description: No brand_rule rows were produced for this blob at all. The five units describe at least two distinct governed concepts (wave graphic construction/usage/colorways, and the masthead gradient+logo device) that must be split into separate rule clusters rather than left unclustered.

rules.summary: Wave graphic element: colorways, construction (70% transparency layering), and usage
Masthead gradient with logo mark as approved device for masthead copy

findings.description: The construction rule states 'always preserve the layered, translucent quality' — a mandatory (hard) directive with an explicit specification (70% transparency, specific color pairing order). No rule captures this hardness=required governance facet; without it the constraint could be misread as a suggestion.

payload.statement: Top wave layer must be rendered at 70% transparency over the base wave (Gold over Sunshine; Sky Blue over Deep Blue); layered translucent quality must always be preserved.

findings.description: 'Approved color values only' is a restrictive compliance clause (scope=color values, polarity=negative/restrictive) that is not captured as a discrete governance facet alongside the general usage guidance.

payload.statement: Only approved color values may be used for wave graphic elements, whether used alone or overlaid on photography/gradient backgrounds.

findings.description: Masthead gradient rule specifies an exact CMYK value (0C,10M,78Y,0K) and mandatory placement of the logo mark at the lightest end — these are hard, specific governance requirements not represented as a rule with hardness/scope facets.

payload.statement: Masthead gradient (Sunshine → 0C,10M,78Y,0K) with logo mark placed at the lightest end is the only approved device for holding copy in masthead positions.
